title: Bridge Paper: Association of Habitual Checking Behaviors on Social Media With Longitudinal Functional Brain Development.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Habit — automaticity, habitual behavior, action monitoring, loss of conscious control.
**Phase context:** habit, automaticity, habitual behavior, action monitoring, chunked behavior

Paper: Association of Habitual Checking Behaviors on Social Media With Longitudinal Functional Brain Development.
DOI: 10.1001/jamapediatrics.2022.4924
Abstract: IMPORTANCE: Social media platforms provide adolescents with unprecedented opportunities for social interactions during a critical developmental period when the brain is especially sensitive to social feedback. OBJECTIVE: To explore how adolescents' frequency of checking behaviors on social media platforms is associated with longitudinal changes in functional brain development across adolescence. DESIGN, SETTING, AND PARTICIPANTS: A 3-year longitudinal cohort study of functional magnetic resonanc
